ranked_movies.title: Pride and Prejudice
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as a tender romance with no toxicity, and specifically mentioned as a favorite in several threads.

ranked_movies.title: The Proposal
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently suggested as a top rom-com, praised for its dynamic between a powerful female lead and an ordinary man, and listed in multiple recommendation threads.

ranked_movies.title: Notting Hill
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended as a classic romance film, often cited for its story of a famous actress and a humble bookshop owner.

ranked_movies.title: About Time
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for its romantic and heartfelt story, though noted to be on the edge regarding cheating themes.

ranked_movies.title: Crazy, Stupid, Love
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times as a well-liked rom-com that balances humor and romance effectively.

ranked_movies.title: The Holiday
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested in several threads as a light-hearted, feel-good romance perfect for date nights or cozy viewing.

ranked_movies.title: When Harry Met Sally
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a quintessential rom-com, praised for its exploration of friendship and love.

ranked_movies.title: Long Shot
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a fresh and hilarious take on the romance genre, with a powerful female lead and an ordinary man.

ranked_movies.title: Roman Holiday
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a classic film where a princess falls for a journalist, often cited for its timeless romance.

ranked_movies.title: The American President
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a political romance with strong performances, and suggested in multiple threads for its dynamic between a lobbyist and the president.

ranked_movies.title: Two Weeks Notice
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a fun rom-com with Sandra Bullock and Hugh Grant, noted for its emotional dynamics and comedic elements.

ranked_movies.title: The Shape of Water
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a favorite romance film for its unique and odd love story, resonating with viewers who relate to the protagonist.

ranked_movies.title: Downsizing
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed extensively for its strong first half and disappointing second half, making it a notable mention in the 'mid-movie collapse' thread.

ranked_movies.title: Spectre
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted in the 'mid-movie collapse' discussion for its engaging first half and disappointing second half, particularly underusing Christoph Waltz.

ranked_movies.title: In Time
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its fantastic concept and strong first half, but criticized for its second half decline into a Bonnie and Clyde narrative.

ranked_movies.title: The Island
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its strong first half and shift into a prolonged chase sequence in the second half, discussed in the 'mid-movie collapse' thread.

ranked_movies.title: Hancock
ranked_movies.reason: Criticized for its mid-movie plot twist that changes the tone drastically, making it a key example of a film that starts strong but falls apart.

ranked_movies.title: The Fall Guy
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a fun action-romance film where the male lead does most of the chasing, and mentioned in multiple threads.

ranked_movies.title: The Kiss Quotient
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a ideal romance novel adaptation, with specific casting ideas discussed in the RomanceBooks thread.

ranked_movies.title: The Princess Bride
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a classic romance-adventure film, perfect for date nights and widely loved for its charm and humor.
